q: If two lines intersect at a point, then the vertically opposite angles are always:
sol: Vertically opposite angles formed by intersecting lines are always equal.

q: Two angles are called supplementary if the sum of their measures is:
sol: The definition of supplementary angles is that they sum up to 180 degrees.

q: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points is called a:
sol: A transversal is a line that crosses at least two other lines.

q: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, each pair of alternate interior angles is:
sol: Alternate interior angles are equal when the lines are parallel.

q: The measure of an angle which is equal to its complement is:
If one angle of a linear pair is acute, then the other angle must be:
sol: Since they sum to 180°, if one is less than 90°, the other must be more than 90°.

q: Two lines in a plane which do not intersect at any point are called:
sol: Parallel lines never meet regardless of how far they are extended.

q: The sum of interior angles on the same side of a transversal is 180° if the lines are:
sol: Consecutive interior angles are supplementary only for parallel lines.

sol: They are the non-adjacent angles formed by intersection.

q: If two lines are parallel to the same line, then they are:
sol: Lines parallel to the same line are parallel to each other.

sol: Concurrent lines pass through the same point.
